channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Israel blocks humanitarian aid into Gaza after Hamas rejects ceasefire extension proposal
item.description: Israel has cut all goods and supplies into the Gaza Strip and warned Hamas it would face "additional consequences" if it does not accept a new deal for an extended ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Uruguay's new leftist president takes office, facing a financial balancing act
item.description: MONTEVIDEO, Uruguay (AP) — Yamandú Orsi, a telegenic left-leaning former mayor and history teacher, took office as Uruguay's new president on Saturday, at the helm of a government that has pledged to strengthen the social safety net while reversing years of economic stagnation.
item.dc:creator: MATILDE CAMPODONICO and ISABEL DEBRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Syrians begin fasting during first Ramadan without Assad family rule in decades
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — Some restaurants and coffee shops in Syria were closed during the day Saturday while others opened as usual as observant Muslims began fasting during the holy month of Ramadan, the first since the fall of Assad family rule in the war-torn country.
item.dc:creator: ABDULRAHMAN SHAHEEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK, France and Ukraine agree to work on ceasefire plan for Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer says Britain, France and Ukraine have agreed to work on a ceasefire plan to present to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Brian Melley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: In address to congress, Argentine President Milei promises IMF deal and lauds economic wins
item.description: Argentina’s libertarian president Javier Milei has signaled that a new and much-anticipated deal with the International Monetary Fund is imminent.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Debre, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel supports a US proposal to extend Gaza ceasefire's first phase. But Hamas wants Phase 2
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel’s government said early Sunday it supports a proposal to extend the first phase of the ceasefire in Gaza through Ramadan and Passover, though Hamas has insisted on negotiating the truce’s second phase instead.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Taiwan’s volunteer military shrinks amid growing Chinese aggression
item.description: Taiwan's military continues to struggle with recruiting troops as it looks to new methods to boost troop strength. One such idea is to enlist migrants into the island's armed forces.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zelenskyy embraced by British prime minister a day after White House blowout
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has told Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy he has the nation’s unwavering support a day after the blowout at the White House with President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Brian Melley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Vatican gives health update as Pope enters third week in hospital
item.description: Pope Francis' condition is stable, and he is alternating between mechanical ventilation and high-flow oxygen therapy, the Vatican said on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukrainians rally around Zelenskyy as defender of national interest after Oval Office blowout
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Soon after Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy left the White House on Friday after an astonishing Oval Office blowout with President Donald Trump, Ukrainians rallied around Zelenskyy as a defender of his country's interests.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia revels in Oval Office spectacle after Zelenskyy spars with Trump, Vance
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's heated exchange with U.S. President Donald Trump has Russian leaders reveling in the spectacle, with one saying Kyiv got a "slap down.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Palestinian businessman lobbies DC with long-shot Gaza vision
item.description: A Palestinian businessman is making a long-shot bid to lead a new governing body of the Gaza Strip, putting up an estimated $300,000 for lobbying services to influence Washington and the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Zelensky won’t apologize for Oval Office spat, says it was bad for both sides
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky wouldn’t apologize for the meeting with President Trump, which erupted into a remarkable and tense spat, arguing that the situation was bad for both countries.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Getting the Israel-Hamas ceasefire to the next phase will be difficult. Here's why
item.description: Israel and Hamas have begun working to advance their ceasefire agreement in Gaza to the next phase, but it's unclear if they'll get there and, if not, what comes next.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lebanon seizes $2.5M destined for Hezbollah after stopping man at airport in 'unprecedented’ bust
item.description: A man allegedly carrying $2.5 million in cash destined for Hezbollah reportedly was detained at Beirut’s airport in what an expert is calling an "unprecedented" bust.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Violent protests erupt in Greece on anniversary of deadly train crash
item.description: A protest over the Greek government’s response to the nation’s largest ever train crash turned violent on Friday as Scores of youths hurled rocks and gasoline bombs at police.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope suffers breathing crisis amid pneumonia battle, Vatican says
item.description: Following the episode, the pope began non-invasive ventilation and was responding well, according to the Vatican.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Myanmar online news site says its jailed journalist is tortured for revealing abuses of prisoners
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — An independent online news agency in Myanmar said Friday that one of its journalists arrested two years ago has been subjected to daily physical and mental abuse after exposing human rights violations in the country’s main prison where he's being held.
item.dc:creator: GRANT PECK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Philippines shark attack: 2 tourists killed at popular diving spot
item.description: Two tourists reportedly were killed by sharks in the Philippines near Verde Island after getting swept away by a current while diving at a popular location
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US Embassy in Thailand warns Americans of 'violent retaliatory attacks' risk after Uyghurs deported to China
item.description: The U.S. Embassy in Bangkok is warning Americans of potential "violent retaliatory attacks" after a group of Uyghurs were deported by Thailand to China.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis' condition improving but will remain 'guarded' until he is stable for several days: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis is improving after spending the past two weeks hospitalized with double pneumonia, but the Vatican said his prognosis is "guarded" until he's stable for a few more days.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A good rapport but mixed signals on Ukraine: Takeaways from Keir Starmer's trip to Washington
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Prime Minister Keir Starmer wore a broad smile aboard the plane home from Washington. He landed back in Britain on Friday with the satisfaction of a tricky mission not quite accomplished, but off to a flying start.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump, Zelenskyy to meet for key deal as NATO allies, Russia wait, watch
item.description: The world waits to see what could come from a key deal between Ukrainian President Zelenskyy and President Trump that could open Kyiv's minerals to the U.S. in exchange for security.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico extradites dozens of cartel leaders and members to US, including drug lord Rafael Caro Quintero
item.description: As Mexico and the U.S. negotiate trade tariffs, the Mexican government has handed over 29 cartel leaders and members who were wanted in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US wants to end Ukraine war with little involvement: Ex-ambassador
item.description: President Donald Trump has met with European leaders during the week to discuss ending the war quickly.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis continues to improve but his prognosis remains guarded: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis continued his slow recovery from double pneumonia on Thursday, beating back speculation of an imminent death, resignation or conclave and signalling that he was still very much in charge albeit in a weakened state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy expected to sign minerals deal Friday: Trump
item.description: Details are vague but what is known is that the United States is requesting revenue from Ukraine's rare minerals.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: US and Ukraine near an economic deal with mineral rights but no security promise, officials say
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine and the U.S. have reached an agreement on a framework for a broad economic deal that would include access to Ukraine's rare earth minerals, three senior Ukrainian officials said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: SUSIE BLANN, HANNA ARHIROVA and VASILISA STEPANENKO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis is critical but stable with no new respiratory crises: Vatican
item.description: “In the morning, after receiving the Eucharist, he resumed work activities,” the Vatican statement said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Bill O'Reilly: Trump wants Nobel Peace Prize via Ukraine peace deal
item.description: O'Reilly defended Trump's approach to ending the Russia-Ukraine conflict, which has entered its third year.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: US refuses to blame Russia for Ukraine war, splitting with European allies in UN votes
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — In a dramatic shift in transatlantic relations under President Donald Trump, the United States split with its European allies by refusing to blame Russia for its invasion of Ukraine in votes on three U.N. resolutions Monday seeking an end to the three-year war.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Western leaders visit Kyiv and pledge military support against Russia on the war's 3rd anniversary
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine on Monday marked the bleakest anniversary yet of its war against the Russia invasion, with the country's forces under severe pressure on the battlefield and U.S. President Donald Trump's administration apparently embracing the Kremlin in a reversal of American policy.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russia-Ukraine war enters third year
item.description: As the war in Ukraine enters its third year, the United States is ramping up efforts to broker a peace deal, but sharp divisions are emerging over how to approach Russia’s role in the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Reshad Hudson, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis in critical condition with early kidney failure but remains alert as prayers pour in
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in critical condition Sunday and blood tests showed early kidney failure but he remains alert, responsive and attended Mass, the Vatican said, as the 88-year-old pontiff battles pneumonia and a complex lung infection.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Conservative opposition wins German election and the far right is 2nd with strongest postwar result
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — The opposition conservatives led by Friedrich Merz won a lackluster victory in Germany's election Sunday and Alternative for Germany doubled its support in the strongest showing for a far-right party since World War II, projections showed.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says he'd be ready to give up presidency if it brought peace, NATO membership
item.description: “If to achieve peace, you really need me to give up my post, I’m ready,” Zelenskyy said in response to a journalist's question on whether he’d trade his office for peace. “I can trade it for NATO.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis remains in 'critical condition' with signs of early kidney failure
item.description: Pope Francis remains hospitalized in "critical condition" with pneumonia, but the Vatican says he rested well overnight.
item.dc:creator: Caitlyn Becker, NewsNation
